purpose: Enumerate and authority-bound all permitted merge actors. Unclassified actors produce MERGE_LEGITIMACY_NULL.

permitted_human_roles.description: Repository maintainer with merge authority on governed branches via approved PR flow
Repository administrator. Merge via governed PR flow is HUMAN_MERGE_ACTOR. Admin bypass of required checks is BREAK_GLASS and does not create merge legitimacy.

sovereignty_review_entry_schema.description: Each entry in sovereignty_review must be an object with login and scope_paths. Workflows read .login for membership and .scope_paths for AC5 coverage.
sovereignty_review_entry_schema.login: string — GitHub login of the authorized sovereignty reviewer
scope_paths: array of glob patterns — files this reviewer is authorized to review (e.g. governance/**, .github/workflows/**)

authority_roles.note: Sovereignty-review authority roles are empty until explicitly populated by repository administrator. Each entry must carry both a login and scope_paths (glob patterns). P3-risk PRs require at least one reviewer whose login is in sovereignty_review AND whose scope_paths cover all changed P3 files (AC5: ChangedP3 ⊆ reviewer.scope.paths). Reviewer in allowlist but with scope_paths that do not cover all changed P3 files produces LEGITIMACY_NULL with failure_reason p3_files_outside_reviewer_scope. An empty allowlist means LEGITIMACY_NULL for all P3 PRs.

single_contributor_policy.description: Single-contributor exception: repository owner may self-certify changes when no peer reviewer is available. Applies only when there is no approved review from any non-author — stale reviews still resolve LEGITIMACY_NULL. Self-certified merges produce OWNER_SELF_CERTIFIED review_status, which is distinct from peer-reviewed APPROVED and is recorded as such in all proof artifacts.
single_contributor_policy.note: OWNER_SELF_CERTIFIED is not equivalent to peer review. It records that a declared single-contributor owner explicitly self-certified the change in the absence of any peer reviewers.

forbidden_actor_patterns.description: Actions using elevated-scope personal access tokens (PATs with admin or write scope) not registered as a permitted bot actor
Any bot actor (login ending in [bot]) not explicitly listed in permitted_bot_actors

classification_rules: Human actors classified by role, not individual login
Bot actors require explicit allowlist entry in permitted_bot_actors
Admin bypass of required checks is classified as BREAK_GLASS on merge proof — merge legitimacy remains NULL
Empty permitted_bot_actors means no bots are permitted merge actors
